text:
Atelier Classroom AI

Founder: Clara Nguyen (ex-teacher). Building an arts-focused classroom copilot (lesson prompts, rubric generation, student feedback). Inbound came from blog post about low-latency copilots. Currently prototyping with hosted API to avoid infra ops - wants self-serve credit + small proof-of-concept. Budget constrained: "can't exceed $1k/mo during pilot" - cost is primary blocker. Careful about moderation: must detect and redact student PII, flag content before teacher view. Wants model to be lightweight (open-model ok) but wants Redwood performance profile and cost visibility. Latency expectations: interactive teacher chat, target p50 < 300ms for short prompts; throughput modest (10-50 reqs/min). "Would rather pay per-use than run infra" - explicit. Uses Google Workspace SSO; asks about SAML timeline. Needs audit logs for teacher actions, basic retention controls (30-90 days). Founder asked for sample SLA language and a sketch of rollout steps for 100-500 classrooms. Quote from call: "We need something that just works for teachers - no ops team." POC success metric: teacher time saved >=20% on lesson prep.
2026-02-20: inbound signup via hosted API (free tier) - founder created account, tested text-generation endpoint
2026-02-24: intro call (30m) with Maya; demo of teacher-facing copilot (screenshare)
2026-02-26: security questionnaire sent by email (asked about SOC2, retention, SSO)
2026-03-01: follow-up message: asked for sample moderation policy mapping; requested price per 1M tokens estimate
2026-03-04: SE sync scheduled for 2026-03-09 to review batching and cost controls; request for 30-day trial credits approved internally

summary: Inbound via PLG trial: signed up self-serve, created API key 2026-03-01, sandbox tests completed.
text:
Custscribe Nexus

Inbound via PLG trial: signed up self-serve, created API key 2026-03-01, sandbox tests completed.
Profile: mid-stage SMB building an automated ticket triage + summarization layer that writes back to Zendesk using tool-calls.
Primary ask: generate 1-3 sentence ticket summaries + structured JSON with fields (priority, probable-intent, suggested-tag, templated-reply).
Latency/throughput: target ~500ms - 2s per ticket for 128-256 token summaries; peak batch ingestion ~30-60 reqs/sec during hourly spikes.
Cost sensitivity: high — evaluating hosted API vs. cheap quantized self-hosted. Looking for predictable per-ticket unit economics.
Model preference: open / cost-efficient models OK; wants Redwood catalog recommendations and explicit fallback routing for capacity shortages.
Security: SSO for team, audit logs for each API call, retention controls (auto-delete raw payload after 30 days) — quoted: 'we can't keep PII longer than 30d',
POC status: pulled test data from sandbox Zendesk export, implemented basic prompt templates, validated structured JSON output for 80% of cases; failure modes around ambiguous multi-issue tickets.
Recent activity (timeline):
- 2026-02-28: inbound signup (organic, developer guide).
- 2026-03-01: sandbox API key issued; first smoke tests: summary + JSON schema validated on 50 tickets.
- 2026-03-04: discovery call with Jordan (AE) + Maya (SE). Fireflies: ff_20260304_8b2f1a.
- 2026-03-06: shared pricing deck and security FAQ (drive links).
- 2026-03-10: engineering note — observed hallucination on billing-related tickets; requested model tuning suggestions.
Sales notes / call snippets:
- 'We want structured output we can plug directly into Zendesk macros' — Customer
- 'If you can show predictable cost per-ticket at scale, we'll convert from trial' — Head of Ops
Open technical questions:
- How to best implement tool-calling for: 1) append internal note, 2) change priority, 3) kick off SLA timer?
- Recommended model + temperature profile for minimizing hallucination on invoice/charge disputes?
Next demo ask: showcase Redwood hosted API + SDK sample that returns: {priority, intent, suggested_reply, zendesk_actions[]} and an example tool-call flow to append a private note.
Handoff: SE to run 45-min technical demo with code samples, and provide cost estimate for 50k tickets/mo using suggested batching + caching optimizations.

summary: Topline: mid-market two-sided marketplace for local services. Interested in Redwood Hosted API for quick integration and then a move to Dedicated for consistent p99 latency and data isolation. Primary focus: replace current heuristic reranker with model-based rerank endpoint and run offline scoring on historical logs.
text:
Peakshore Labs

Topline: mid-market two-sided marketplace for local services. Interested in Redwood Hosted API for quick integration and then a move to Dedicated for consistent p99 latency and data isolation. Primary focus: replace current heuristic reranker with model-based rerank endpoint and run offline scoring on historical logs.

Meeting snippets:
- 2025-01-22 demo: AE Maya + SE Owen — showed hosted rerank endpoint (streaming) vs their batch rerank. CTO quote: "need measurable NDCG uplift, can't break latency SLA."
- 2025-02-06 pricing call: product head asked for committed throughput pricing for Dedicated; wants a 3-month POC price and an option to convert to reserved capacity.

What they run today:
- Candidate gen: BM25 + feature filters, then light xgboost re-ranker.
- Offline eval: internal NDCG scripts on 5M labeled impressions / year. Want to run model scoring offline for entire logs (10M candidates/day target).

POC acceptance criteria (customer):
- Offline: 10%+ relative NDCG improvement on heldout eval set (primary).
- Online: p95 latency for rerank scoring < 200ms (target), p99 < 300ms acceptable with Dedicated.
- Cost: unit economics competitive vs current xgboost infra; target < $X per 1M candidates scored (they will compare).
- Integration: simple SDK + ability to run bulk offline scoring (batch job or S3->batch API).

Security/compliance notes:
- Must sign DPA; data residency for EU users (mix of NA + EU). Require SOC2 + SSO/SAML + KMS integration for keys. Audit logs retention + queryable event history required.

Customer sentiment / misc:
- Product team pragmatic, wants clear A/B plan and eval harness before committing.
- Raised concern: how Redwood's reranker handles cold-start items and long-tail specialty queries.
- Wants ability to fallback to cheaper model variant if Dedicated capacity saturated.

Internal playbook notes:
- Offer 2-week hosted pilot (free credits) to generate baseline evals; run offline scoring job on sample month of logs. If uplift looks good, propose 8-week Dedicated POC with committed throughput and SLA. Include SOW + migration checklist.

Key contacts:
- CTO: Lena Ortiz (lena@peakshore.ai) - primary technical signoff
- Head of Product: Ravi Menon (ravi@peakshore.ai) - product/metrics

2025-01-10 - initial inbound via website form (interest: reranking/demo)
2025-01-15 - discovery call (AE Maya + Product) - collected evaluation metrics and current infra
2025-01-22 - hosted API demo + rerank walkthrough (Fireflies: ff_2025-01-22_peakshore-demo)
2025-01-29 - sent integration guide + sample eval harness (drive link)
2025-02-06 - pricing call (requested Dedicated POC pricing)
2025-02-12 - security questionnaire returned (SOC2 attach), asked about data residency options
